body:
## Summary

- Fix `glob` with absolute paths in `FieldTimeSeries` constructors (split directory from pattern)
- Fix `OnDisk` `FieldTimeSeries` with split files by introducing `SplitFilePath` struct that maps global time indices to the correct part file
- Fix checkpointer `restore_prognostic_state!` to update `writer.filepath` to match the restored part number, and save/restore `file_splitting` schedule state
- Add post-split `iteration_exists` check in `write_output!` to skip already-written iterations after pickup
- Add tests for split file reading (InMemory + OnDisk) and checkpointer pickup with file splitting

**Depends on #5486** — that PR must be merged first since this branch builds on top of `ss/grids-in-the-field`.

## Test plan

- [x] Verify `FieldTimeSeries` with absolute paths and split files works for both `InMemory` and `OnDisk` backends
- [x] Verify checkpointer pickup correctly restores filepath and file splitting schedule
- [x] Verify "moved-away parts" scenario: only latest part file remains, pickup appends correctly
- [x] Run existing test suite to check for regressions

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

labels.description: Even a perfect program still has bugs
labels.name: bug 🐞

title: (0.107.0) Fix file splitting for JLD2Writer: OnDisk FTS, checkpointer pickup, and glob

body:
## Summary

- Fixes catastrophic cancellation in Float32 WENO smoothness indicators (β) that produced **negative β values** and incorrect weights, causing NaN crashes with high-order WENO schemes
- The β quadratic form `ψᵀCψ` is invariant to constant shifts (it measures polynomial derivatives). Subtracting the stencil mean before the computation reduces operand magnitudes from O(ψ) to O(Δψ), eliminating the cancellation
- A dispatch on `NTuple{N, Float32}` ensures **zero overhead for Float64**

Before (raw Float32, ρθ ≈ 340 stencil):
```
β = [3.01e-1, 9.67e-2, 3.42e-2, -1.76e-2, 1.56e-1]   ← negative β, 1180% error
ω = [0.000, 0.000, 0.984, 0.000, 0.016]                ← nearly ENO
```

After (mean-subtracted Float32):
```
β = [2.72e-1, 6.37e-2, 2.03e-2, 1.24e-2, 1.22e-2]     ← matches Float64
ω = [0.000, 0.035, 0.499, 0.443, 0.023]                 ← correct WENO weights
```

Closes #5485

## Test plan

- [x] Existing WENO tests pass (Float64 path is unchanged — identity fallback)
- [x] Float32 WENO reconstruction tests produce correct weights for large-mean stencils
- [x] Float32 WENO9 neutral ABL simulation (previously crashed at ~25 min) runs stably

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
